security_deposit.allowed_deductions_rule: - Permissible deductions: Unpaid rent, damage beyond normal wear and tear, other amounts owed under the lease.
security_deposit.penalty_for_bad_faith_rule: - Penalty: If landlord fails to return deposit within 45 days without legitimate deductions, tenant may recover actual damages, costs, and attorney's fees.
security_deposit.deposit_storage_segregation_rule: - Separate account: Landlord is required to hold security deposit in a separate account in a Mississippi banking institution, or post a surety bond.
security_deposit.special_rules_local_overrides_note: Local ordinances and special housing programs can add stricter rules.

eviction.nonpayment_notice_type: - Nonpayment of rent: 3 days written notice to quit. If tenant pays overdue rent plus any costs before judgment, tenancy may be reinstated.
eviction.lease_violation_notice_type: - Lease violation: Generally handled per lease terms; courts enforce lease provisions.
eviction.holdover_notice_type: - Month-to-month termination: 30 days written notice required.
eviction.court_process_summary_rule: - Court process: Landlord files in justice court (for amounts typically up to $3,500) or county court; tenant served; hearing within days.
eviction.filing_court_rule: - Court process: Landlord files in justice court (for amounts typically up to $3,500) or county court; tenant served; hearing within days.
eviction.service_of_process_rule: - Court process: Landlord files in justice court (for amounts typically up to $3,500) or county court; tenant served; hearing within days.

landlord_entry.allowed_entry_reasons_rule: - Entry for repairs: Landlord may enter to make repairs, deliver items, or inspect the premises. - Entry to show: Landlord may enter to show premises to prospective tenants, buyers, or lenders.
landlord_entry.entry_time_window_rule: - Entry times: Entry should be at reasonable times.
landlord_entry.penalty_for_illegal_entry_rule: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

rent_increase.fixed_term_increase_allowed_rule: - Fixed-term leases: Rent cannot be increased during the lease term.
rent_increase.month_to_month_increase_allowed_rule: - Rent increases: No statutory limit on amount or frequency for month-to-month tenancies.
rent_increase.delivery_method_rule: - Notice required: For month-to-month tenancies, at least 30 days advance written notice.
rent_increase.retaliation_protection_note: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

habitability.landlord_repair_duty_rule: - Landlord's duty: Comply with applicable building and housing codes; make repairs to keep premises in good condition; maintain structural, plumbing, heating, electrical systems; provide running water and hot water.
habitability.tenant_termination_for_unrepaired_condit: 3. Termination: Tenant may terminate the rental agreement if conditions substantially impair health or safety and landlord fails to repair after notice.
habitability.code_enforcement_contact_rule: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.
